title: Buck Mason Stylist — customer profile
description: Machine-readable schema for the customer's profile.md (front-matter or YAML body). Agents may load and validate against this so missing fields fail loudly at parse time rather than silently downstream. Sister doc: templates/profile.example.md.

pima_api_key.description: Public Pima API key for /api/* (order tracking, returns). Not used for /mcp/*.

jwt.description: Set automatically after /api/login_via_token; do not fill manually.

link_payment_method.description: Customer's Stripe Link wallet readiness. `confirmed` means a payment method is linked and MPP fully-agent-driven checkout is reachable. `none` = customer doesn't have Link or doesn't want to use it; default to the Shopify cart-link path. `unconfirmed` triggers the agent to ask once on first lookbook.

preferred_lookbook_host.description: Sticky preference set after the first successful publish. When unset, the agent runs the probe in references/hosting-options.md and asks once.

preferred_lookbook_host_auto.description: When true, the agent skips per-publish confirmation for the customer's preferred host. Required for headless / cron mode (references/headless-mode.md).

cloudflare_pages_project.description: Pages project name; reused across deploys for a stable alias URL.

s3_bucket.description: Required if preferred_lookbook_host is `s3`. Must be public-read configured.

lookbook_project_prefix.description: Customer-namespaced prefix for permanent-URL Pages projects. Final pattern: `<prefix>-<lookbook-id>`. Default if unset: `buckmason-<email-handle>`. See references/hosting-options.md § "URL stability".

weekly_lookbook_cadence.description: Recurring newsletter cadence. `off` = recurring lookbooks disabled (event-driven still runs per references/event-suitability.md). See references/headless-mode.md § "Recurring weekly newsletter".

weekly_lookbook_tier.description: Editorial = product imagery + flat-lays (no AI try-on, no OpenAI cost). Premium = gpt-image-2 try-on per look (~$0.40/run). Default `editorial` for recurring runs to avoid per-week spend.

notify_url.description: Where headless-mode runs post their summary (ntfy.sh topic, Slack incoming webhook, etc). Optional — falls back to a local summary file under ~/.buck-mason-stylist/runs/<lookbook_id>/summary.md when unset.

calendar_scoring.description: Whether the agent applies references/event-suitability.md to the customer's calendar. Off until the customer explicitly connects a calendar source.

build.description: Required for image-gen fidelity (premium tier). Optional otherwise.

height.description: Imperial or metric, e.g. "6'2\"" or "185 cm"

beard.description: Free-text but follow examples: clean-shaven / stubble / short beard / full beard / mustache

sizes.description: Required. Letter sizes for tops, waist or waist x inseam for pants.

pant.description: Waist or waist x inseam, e.g. "31" or "32x32"

shoe.description: US men's or women's, half-sizes allowed

^cut_overrides$.description: Per-cut overrides when the customer's size differs from the default for a specific Buck Mason cut, e.g. {boxy_tee: "XL"}

style_ethos.description: 8–14 word tag the agent uses to bias classic vs trend, e.g. "relaxed European cool — neutral palette, linen camp collars, tobacco suede loafers". See references/style-reasoning.md.

shipping_address.description: Required for MPP fully-agent-driven checkout. Sent to /mcp/buckmason/checkout in the buyer block.

home_zip.description: Required for `stores near me` proximity sort.

reference_photos.description: Identity anchors for AI try-on. ≥2 required for premium-tier lookbook; fewer triggers fallback to editorial tier.

path.description: Absolute filesystem path or URL the agent can read.

typical_outfit_budget.description: Default total dollars per outfit when not specified. Maps to /mcp/recommend?budget=.

max_per_item.description: Never recommend a single piece over this without confirming. Maps to /mcp/recommend?max_price_per_item=.

notes.description: Free-text reminders the agent should weight when picking.
